name: Ormston House
content: Ormston House is a flexible gallery crossed with an arts space. The open hours are a little limited, but I would highly recommend trying to catch an open hours or an event here. All of the exhibitions are interesting, and are usually very recent or local artists. I got to see Lori Goldstone perform there, who, unbeknownst to me at the time, was the cellist on Nirvana’s MTV Unplugged in New York. Her solo work is incredible as well. I went into the concert assuming I would enjoy myself, and left wondering not what sounds a cello could make, but rather what sounds make a cello. To this day I’m still not sure how she was able to produce the sounds she did with only a cello. You should check out both Ormston House and Lori Goldstone’s work [https://www.lorigoldston.com/].
